summary_markdown:
```markdown
## What this issue is about
This issue suggests enhancing the Volto application by adding a Locking Control Panel. Currently, site administrators can release locks on objects using the Zope Management Interface (ZMI), but having this feature in Volto will improve usability and reduce reliance on the classic interface.

## What needs to be done
- Implement REST API endpoints to retrieve locked content.
- Create a Volto control panel that displays all locks on a site, allows filtering by path, and provides options to release locks on one or more items.

## Helpful background
Locks are used to prevent changes to objects in Plone. Currently, this management is accessible through ZMI, but the aim is to make it more user-friendly with a modern interface. While the unlock feature works, the approach for releasing non-stealable locks needs clarification.

## Good first steps
1. Familiarize yourself with existing REST API implementations in Volto.
2. Review the current ZMI functionality about locks to understand what is needed in the Volto interface.
3. Consider proposing how to handle non-stealable locks if you have ideas.
4. Feel free to ask questions if anything is unclear; community feedback is welcomed!
```
